system.vehicleTraits: Enclosed, Environmentally Sealed, Enhanced Auspex Arrays, Fear (3), Reinforced Armour, Walker, Super-heavy, Ion Shield (PR 50), Strikedown, Armour and War-Machine
system.pilotTalents: Lightning Reflexes, Weapon Training (Low-tech, SP, Las), Sure Strike, Called Shot, Rite of Becoming, Air of Authority, Peer (Nobility)
system.history: The Knight Paladin is the most common Knight configuration, bearing a rapid-fire battle cannon and reaper chainsword. Excels at long-range fire support.
system.specialRules: Master of the Hunt (1/combat: reroll failed shooting attack). Carapace Mounting available.

system.vehicleTraits: Enclosed, Environmentally Sealed, Enhanced Auspex Arrays, Fear (3), Reinforced Armour, Walker, Super-heavy, Ion Shield (PR 50), Strikedown, Armour and War-Machine
system.pilotTalents: Lightning Reflexes, Weapon Training (Low-tech, SP, Las), Sure Strike, Called Shot, Rite of Becoming, Air of Authority, Peer (Nobility)
system.history: The Knight Errant bears a thermal cannon capable of melting tanks at close range. A devastating assault knight.
system.specialRules: Master of the Melee (1/combat: reroll failed melee attacks). Carapace Mounting available.

system.vehicleTraits: Enclosed, Environmentally Sealed, Enhanced Auspex Arrays, Fear (3), Reinforced Armour, Walker, Super-heavy, Ion Shield (PR 50), Strikedown, Armour and War-Machine
system.pilotTalents: Lightning Reflexes, Weapon Training (Low-tech, SP, Las), Sure Strike, Called Shot, Rite of Becoming, Air of Authority, Peer (Nobility)
system.history: The Knight Warden's Avenger Gatling Cannon tears apart infantry formations while the co-axial heavy flamer ensures nothing in cover survives.
system.specialRules: Carapace Mounting available. Excels at clearing infantry and storming fortifications.

system.vehicleTraits: Enclosed, Environmentally Sealed, Enhanced Auspex Arrays, Fear (3), Reinforced Armour, Walker, Super-heavy, Ion Shield (PR 50), Strikedown, Armour and War-Machine
system.pilotTalents: Lightning Reflexes, Weapon Training (Low-tech, SP, Las), Sure Strike, Called Shot, Rite of Becoming, Air of Authority, Peer (Nobility)
system.history: The Knight Gallant carries both a Reaper Chainsword and Thunderstrike Gauntlet, making it the most devastating close-combat Knight available.
system.specialRules: Master of the Joust (1/combat: reroll 1s on melee damage). Master of the Melee (1/combat: reroll failed melee). Carapace available. Close combat specialist — +5 WS.

system.vehicleTraits: Enclosed, Environmentally Sealed, Enhanced Auspex Arrays, Fear (3), Reinforced Armour, Walker, Super-heavy, Ion Shield (PR 50), Strikedown, Armour and War-Machine
system.pilotTalents: Lightning Reflexes, Weapon Training (Low-tech, SP, Las), Sure Strike, Called Shot, Rite of Becoming, Air of Authority, Peer (Nobility)
system.history: The Knight Crusader is armed with two main guns — Avenger Gatling Cannon and either Thermal Cannon or RF Battlecannon. A one-Knight spearhead.
system.specialRules: Master of the Hunt (1/combat: reroll failed shooting). Carries more firepower than a tank squadron. +5 BS.

system.vehicleTraits: Enclosed, Environmentally Sealed, Enhanced Auspex Arrays, Fear (3), Reinforced Armour, Walker, Super-heavy, Ion Shield (PR 50), Strikedown, Armour and War-Machine
system.pilotTalents: Lightning Reflexes, Weapon Training (Low-tech, SP, Las), Sure Strike, Called Shot, Rite of Becoming, Air of Authority, Peer (Nobility)
system.history: The Knight Lancer is the fastest Knight variant, designed for devastating charge attacks. Favoured by impetuous Scions who follow the Uhlan tradition.
system.specialRules: Cerastus chassis: superior speed (70kph, 20/40/60/120 tactical). No Carapace mounting. Ion Gauntlet Shield replaces standard Ion Shield. Shock lance usable at range AND in melee.

system.vehicleTraits: Enclosed, Environmentally Sealed, Enhanced Auspex Arrays, Fear (3), Reinforced Armour, Walker, Super-heavy, Ion Shield (PR 50), Strikedown, Armour and War-Machine
system.pilotTalents: Lightning Reflexes, Weapon Training (Low-tech, SP, Las), Sure Strike, Called Shot, Rite of Becoming, Air of Authority, Peer (Nobility)
system.history: The Knight Castigator bears the fearsome bolt cannon and elegant Tempest Warblade. Favoured by veteran Scions who form the core of Household Arbalester orders.
system.specialRules: Cerastus chassis. Tempest Attack with Warblade — at Initiative 2, one automatic hit on ALL combatants in melee range. Deflagrate: each wounding hit spawns extra automatic hit.

system.vehicleTraits: Enclosed, Environmentally Sealed, Enhanced Auspex Arrays, Fear (3), Reinforced Armour, Walker, Super-heavy, Ion Shield (PR 50), Strikedown, Armour and War-Machine
system.pilotTalents: Lightning Reflexes, Weapon Training (Low-tech, SP, Las), Sure Strike, Called Shot, Rite of Becoming, Air of Authority, Peer (Nobility)
system.history: The Knight Acheron is deployed only for missions of extermination. Its animus is saturated with inherited memories of massacres. Scions rarely choose this mount willingly.
system.specialRules: Cerastus chassis. Flame Cannon ignores all cover. Reaper Chainfist has Machine Destroyer and Destroyer[5] — most powerful melee weapon available to Knights. Acheron animus: +10 WP vs Warp, +1d5 Corruption.

system.vehicleTraits: Enclosed, Environmentally Sealed, Enhanced Auspex Arrays, Fear (3), Reinforced Armour, Walker, Super-heavy, Ion Shield (PR 50), Strikedown, Armour and War-Machine
system.pilotTalents: Lightning Reflexes, Sure Strike, Called Shot, Rite of Becoming
system.history: A Knight who has turned from the Emperor's light. Equipment often in poor repair, ammunition supplies exhausted, joints rusted and vulnerable. But still utterly deadly.
system.specialRules: Renegade: -10 to requisition rolls for ammunition and repairs. May have random equipment malfunctions at GM's discretion (vehicle with poorly maintained weapons).

system.vehicleTraits: Enclosed, Environmentally Sealed, Reinforced Armour, Walker, Super-heavy, Strikedown, Damage Control, Effigy (Orks within 30m count as Fearless)
system.history: A massive Ork war machine of crude but devastating construction. The largest commonly encountered Ork walker, dwarfed only by Gargants.
system.specialRules:
Effigy: Orks within 30m count as Fearless.
Titanic size: +40 to be hit in melee and ranged.
Carrying Capacity: 20 Orks.
No Ion Shield — Orks don't do that sort of thing.

system.vehicleTraits: Enclosed, Environmentally Sealed, Reinforced Armour, Walker
system.history: A mid-sized Ork walker, larger than a Dreadnought but smaller than a Stompa. Bristling with Kustom weapons of dubious reliability.
system.specialRules:
Carrying Capacity: 6 Orks.
Kustom weapons: Many have Overheats and Unreliable qualities — rolls of 1 on the attack die may cause malfunction.

system.vehicleTraits: From Beyond (immune to Fear/Pinning/Insanity), Auto-stabilised, Unnatural Strength[12], Unnatural Toughness[12], Unnatural Agility[2], Fear(4), Size[Monumental]
system.pilotTalents: Ambidextrous, Assassin Strike, Blademaster, Catfall, Crushing Blow, Hard Target, True Grit, Preternatural Speed, Sprint
system.history: A massive Eldar wraithbone construct animated by the spirit stone of a dead warrior. Virtually immune to psychological effects and phenomenally agile for its size.
system.specialRules:
Wounds: 100 (use Wounds, not Structural Integrity).
Shimmershield: PR 40 Force Field, overloads on 1-5.
Ghostflight: counts as Jump Pack.
Armour: Wraithbone 20 all facings.
From Beyond: immune to Fear, Pinning, and Insanity.
